items.name: MD SB 881 DIED — no Maryland disclosure law; watch for 2027
items.summary: Verified 6/7/2026 against the official MGA pages: SB 881 passed the Senate 42-0 but died in the House at sine die (Apr 13, 2026); crossfile HB 1007 also died. Maryland has NO commercial financing disclosure law. The unanimous Senate vote makes a 2027 reintroduction highly likely — status moved to WATCH.

items.name: Texas HB 700 confirmed LAW — Fin. Code Ch. 398, effective 9/1/2025
items.summary: Verified 6/7/2026 from capitol.texas.gov: total-cost disclosures (no APR) on commercial sales-based financing offers under $1M. Providers AND brokers must register with the Texas OCCC by Dec 31, 2026 — registration is not capped at $1M. Finance Commission rules pending (due 9/1/2026). Matrix status moved verify → LAW.

items.name: Louisiana confirmed LAW — Act 198 (R.S. 9:3137.10) + 2024 disclosure law
items.summary: Verified 6/7/2026 from legis.la.gov: Act 198 of 2025 (HB 470, eff. Aug 1, 2025) requires total-cost disclosures on revenue-based financing with NO dollar threshold and no exemptions, layered on the 2024 law (R.S. 9:3138.1–3138.6, eff. Jan 1, 2025). No APR requirement, no registration. Matrix status moved verify → LAW.

items.name: CAN-SPAM penalty confirmed: $53,088/email for 2026
items.summary: Verified 6/7/2026: the 2026 inflation adjustment was canceled (OMB Memo M-26-11 — appropriations lapse blocked the Oct 2025 CPI-U), so the 2025 maximum of $53,088 per email carries into 2026.

items.name: CFPB §1071 small business lending rule — revised final rule
items.summary: Seeded at engine launch: CFPB issued a revised final 1071 rule (May 2026) — narrowed scope, single compliance date Jan 1, 2028, ≥1,000 originations/yr threshold. Litigation ongoing; status fluid.

items.name: TCPA — revocation rule in effect; one-to-one consent rule vacated
items.summary: Seeded at engine launch: the FCC revocation rule (STOP-keyword honoring within 10 business days) has been in effect since Apr 2025; the Dec 2023 one-to-one consent rule was VACATED by the 11th Circuit (Jan 2025) — bundled consent permissible again, PEWC still required.
